title: Day 1: How the Web Works
exercise: Explain in your own words what happens when you type a URL into a browser.

title: Day 2: HTML Fundamentals: Structure
exercise: Create a simple HTML page with a heading, a paragraph, and an image.

title: Day 3: HTML: Links and Tables
exercise: Create a table listing your top 5 favorite movies and their release years.

title: Day 4: HTML: Forms and Inputs
exercise: Design a contact form with fields for Name, Email, and Message.

title: Day 5: CSS Basics: Selectors and Colors
exercise: Change the background color and text color of your HTML page from Day 2.

title: Day 6: CSS: The Box Model
exercise: Create a box on your page and experiment with different padding and margin values.

title: Day 7: CSS: Typography and Fonts
exercise: Apply a Google Font to your HTML page and adjust the font-size and line-height.

title: Day 8: CSS: Layout with Flexbox
exercise: Create a horizontal navigation bar using Flexbox.

title: Day 9: CSS: Layout with Grid
exercise: Build a simple 3-column photo gallery using CSS Grid.

title: Day 10: Responsive Design & Media Queries
exercise: Adjust your layout so that the navigation bar turns into a vertical list on small screens.

title: Day 11: Intro to JavaScript: Variables & Logic
exercise: Write a script that displays an alert saying 'Hello, World!' when a button is clicked.

title: Day 12: JS: Functions and Scoping
content: Organizing your code into reusable blocks.
exercise: Create a function that takes two numbers and returns their sum.

title: Day 13: JS: DOM Manipulation
exercise: Create a button that changes the color of a heading when clicked.

title: Day 14: JS: Events and Listeners
exercise: Create a text input that displays the character count as the user types.

title: Day 15: JS: Arrays and Objects
exercise: Create an object representing a 'Playbook' with title, description, and lessons.

title: Day 16: CSS Frameworks Intro (Bootstrap/Tailwind)
exercise: Re-create your navigation bar using a framework's built-in classes.

title: Day 17: Version Control with Git
exercise: Set up a Git repository and commit your first HTML/CSS project.

title: Day 18: Hosting & Deployment
content: Putting your site on the internet.
exercise: Deploy your simple HTML/CSS page to Netlify or Vercel.

title: Day 19: Web Performance Basics
content: How to make your site load fast.
exercise: Optimize one image on your page using an online compressor and compare file sizes.

title: Day 20: Accessibility (a11y)
exercise: Audit your page for alt text on images and proper heading structure.

title: Day 21: Backend Basics (Node.js/APIs)
exercise: Research what JSON is and why it's used for APIs.

title: Day 22: Working with Databases Intro
exercise: List the difference between a SQL and NoSQL database.

title: Day 23: Modern JS Frameworks Intro (React/Vue)
exercise: Find a 'Hello World' React tutorial and read through the first 3 steps.

title: Day 24: SEO for Developers
exercise: Add Meta tags for Title and Description to your HTML page.

title: Day 25: Browser Developer Tools
exercise: Inspect a popular website and change its main heading text using DevTools.

title: Day 26: Web Security Basics
exercise: Explain the difference between HTTP and HTTPS.

title: Day 27: Chrome Extensions & Tools
exercise: Install a developer extension like Wappalyzer or a Color Picker.

title: Day 28: Portfolio Building (Web)
exercise: Draft the 'Portfolio' section of your personal website with links to your Git/Live sites.

title: Day 29: Final Project: Personal Landing Page
exercise: Build a responsive landing page for yourself including an intro, skills, and contact form.

title: Day 30: The Dev Journey
content: Staying current in a fast-paced field.
exercise: Set a goal to learn one specific framework or library (e.g., Next.js) next.
